mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during periods of supply chain bottlenecks or inventory destocking, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 215.0 while the federal funds rate remains below 0.50 percent, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting market pricing of future aggressive tightening and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL rises but FEDFUNDS adjusts rapidly, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal strengthening economic activity and rising wage income, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 0.5 percent quarter-over-quarter, aggregate demand contraction forces corporate earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of low-GDP-growth quarters, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly with t-test p greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and real personal consumption expenditures confirms demand-supply alignment, reducing inventory risk and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts negative returns when supply outpaces demand, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample validation.
